page: OVHcloud r3-512 is a memory-optimized server instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system allocates exactly one thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

In benchmark evaluations, the r3-512 demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory performance is similarly robust, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching the top 10% at 8439.52 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write speeds rank in the top 25%. The server also excels in database operations, achieving a top-tier rating in Redis SET operations with 134.58 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks show moderate performance, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression falling into the middle 50% average tier.

From a resource-density perspective, this instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its large RAM-to-vCPU allocation. The primary hardware tradeoff is the relatively small 400 GB local NVMe storage capacity relative to the 512 GB memory footprint, which may require external storage attachments for data-heavy configurations. Additionally, the lack of hyperthreading limits concurrent thread execution but ensures dedicated, predictable physical core performance. The OVHcloud r3-512 is structured for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: OVHcloud r3-512 is a memory-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at 2.3 GHz with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data places the instance in the top 10% for multi-core processing, Passmark memory scores, and Redis SET operations, though single-threaded compression tasks perform at an average level. The high memory-to-core ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive deployments, offset by a relatively small local storage capacity. This server is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, multi-threaded compilation, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-512 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and top-tier multi-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

512 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
